verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand encoded as a single-pass type I transmembrane proligand. The membrane-anchored precursor is expressed on the cell surface where it can engage in juxtacrine ErbB signaling before being shed by ADAM10/ADAM17 sheddases to release the soluble mature form. The TM precursor stage is real but transient — the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble ligand. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern: contextual/dual_localization rather than yes (stable surface receptor) or secreted_only.
